title: Grade 4 Activities
description: Place value, operations, fractions, decimals, and geometry

level1.title: Place Value, Rounding & Add/Sub Algorithms
level1.goal: Represent, compare, and round numbers to 1,000,000 and add/subtract multi-digit numbers with meaning.

g4-l1-big-idea.title: Big idea
g4-l1-big-idea.content: Multi-digit numbers are built from place values. When kids can *see* a number as hundred-thousands, ten-thousands, thousands, hundreds, tens, and ones, comparing, rounding, and adding/subtracting become much calmer and more reliable.

g4-l1-notice.title: What to notice
g4-l1-notice.content:
* Compares by looking left first (biggest place value).
* Uses zeros as "empty places," not "nothing to ignore."
* Rounds by thinking "which multiple is closer?"
* Checks an exact answer with an estimate.

g4-l1-home.title: Try at home
g4-l1-home.content:
* **Game A: "Build a Number"** Write digits 0-9 on cards. Draw a place-value chart labeled hundred-thousands, ten-thousands, thousands, hundreds, tens, and ones. Draw six cards to build the biggest number, the smallest number, and a number close to 500,000.
* **Game B: "Estimate Then Check"** Pick two prices or counts from real life (groceries, steps, views). Round each, add/subtract the rounded numbers, then compare to the exact total.

g4-l1-videos.title: Optional videos
g4-l1-videos.content: **Search on YouTube:** * place value to one million (grade 4) * rounding multi-digit numbers to any place * addition & subtraction with regrouping (grade 4) **Extra practice:** * Khan Academy: 4th grade (Common Core)

level2.title: Factors, Multiples & Multi-Digit Multiply/Divide
level2.goal: Use structure (factors/multiples) and models to multiply and divide larger numbers, then connect to efficient methods.

g4-l2-big-idea.title: Big idea
g4-l2-big-idea.content: Multiplication and division are about *groups* and *arrays*. The area model and partial products show why the algorithms work, so "carrying" and "bringing down" don't feel like magic.

g4-l2-notice.title: What to notice
g4-l2-notice.content:
* Can find factor pairs by making rectangles.
* Uses distributive thinking (20 x 14 plus 3 x 14).
* In division, checks by multiplying the quotient back.
* Treats remainders based on the story (leftovers or "need another box").

g4-l2-home.title: Try at home
g4-l2-home.content:
* **Game A: "Rectangle Detective"** Choose a number (24, 30, 36). Find all rectangles you can build with small objects or drawn grids. Each rectangle is a factor pair.
* **Game B: "Shopping Packs"** Pretend you buy 128 stickers in packs of 6. How many full packs? How many left? Would you buy one extra pack?

g4-l2-videos.title: Optional videos
g4-l2-videos.content: **Search on YouTube:** * area model for 2-digit x 2-digit * partial quotients division (grade 4) * factors and multiples (grade 4) **Extra curriculum links:** * Illustrative Mathematics: Grade 4 units * Eureka Math: Grade 4 module overview (PDF)

level3.title: Fractions & Mixed Numbers
level3.goal: Understand fractions as numbers, find equivalent fractions, compare them, and add/subtract with like denominators.

g4-l3-big-idea.title: Big idea
g4-l3-big-idea.content: Fractions are numbers with a *location* (number line) and a *size*. Equivalent fractions are simply different names for the same size. When denominators match, adding and subtracting is about combining or removing equal parts.

g4-l3-notice.title: What to notice
g4-l3-notice.content:
* Places fractions correctly between 0 and 1 (or beyond 1).
* Knows that multiplying top and bottom by the same number keeps the value the same.
* Uses benchmarks (1/2, 1) instead of guessing.
* With like denominators, add/subtract the numerators while keeping the denominator.

g4-l3-home.title: Try at home
g4-l3-home.content:
* **Game A: "Fraction Strip Race"** Draw a strip for 1 whole. Fold it (or mark) into 2, 3, 4, 6, 8 equal parts. Find pairs that are equal sizes (1/2 = 2/4 = 4/8).
* **Game B: "Recipe Double"** Pick a simple recipe step: "Use 2/3 cup." Ask: "What is 2 x 2/3?" Use a measuring cup picture or draw the fraction.

g4-l3-videos.title: Optional videos
g4-l3-videos.content: Search on YouTube: * equivalent fractions with models * add/sub fractions with like denominators * mixed numbers and improper fractions Problem-solving extensions: * NRICH: Exploring Fractions (tasks) * YouCubed: Fraction Puzzles

level4.title: Decimals, Measurement & Data
level4.goal: Connect fractions to decimals (tenths/hundredths), convert units within a system, and solve measurement/data problems.

g4-l4-big-idea.title: Big idea
g4-l4-big-idea.content: Decimals are another way to name fractions with denominators 10 and 100. Measurement problems get easier when kids keep track of *units* and use conversions as "multiply or divide by a known factor."

g4-l4-notice.title: What to notice
g4-l4-notice.content:
* Reads 0.4 as "four tenths," not "zero point four."
* Compares decimals by tenths first, then hundredths.
* Writes units with answers (cm, in, m, ft).
* Distinguishes area (square units) from perimeter (units).

g4-l4-home.title: Try at home
g4-l4-home.content:
* **Game A: "Hundred Grid Art"** Shade 0.36, 0.5, 0.08 on hundred grids (10x10). Write each as a fraction over 100, and over 10 when possible.
* **Game B: "Measure + Convert"** Measure a table edge in inches. Convert to feet and inches. Then measure in centimeters and compare.

g4-l4-videos.title: Optional videos
g4-l4-videos.content: Search on YouTube: * tenths and hundredths decimals * unit conversions (grade 4) * area and perimeter (grade 4) Optional programs: * Math in Focus (Singapore Math approach)

level5.title: Angles & Shape Properties
level5.goal: Measure and draw angles, understand lines and symmetry, and classify shapes by properties.

g4-l5-big-idea.title: Big idea
g4-l5-big-idea.content: Geometry is about *relationships*: how lines meet, how angles measure "turn," and how shapes can be sorted by properties. When students can name properties, they can explain *why* a shape belongs in a category.

g4-l5-notice.title: What to notice
g4-l5-notice.content:
* Recognizes right angles even when rotated.
* Measures angles by aligning the baseline first.
* Uses "parallel" as "never meet" and "perpendicular" as "make right angles."
* Classifies a square as a special rectangle (and a special rhombus).

g4-l5-home.title: Try at home
g4-l5-home.content:
* **Game A: "Angle Hunt"** Find 5 angles in your home (book corner, door hinge, table). Label each as acute/right/obtuse.
* **Game B: "Fold Symmetry"** Cut out simple shapes from paper. Fold to test for symmetry lines. Draw the fold lines.

g4-l5-videos.title: Optional videos
g4-l5-videos.content: Search on YouTube: * measuring angles with a protractor * parallel and perpendicular lines * classifying quadrilaterals Challenge puzzles: * Beast Academy Level 4 (advanced/puzzle style)

place-value-to-million.title: Place Value to 1,000,000 Telescope
place-value-to-million.description: Build big numbers using a place-value chart.
place-value-to-million.instruction: Build the number shown using the place-value chart!

expanded-form-million.title: Expanded Form Million Match
expanded-form-million.description: Match a number to its expanded form.
expanded-form-million.instruction: Match each number to its expanded form!
expanded-form-million.selectPair: Select a number, then select its expanded form!

compare-order-big.title: Compare & Order Big Numbers
compare-order-big.instruction: Compare the numbers using >, <, or = then order them!
compare-order-big.orderPrompt: Put these numbers in order from least to greatest!
compare-order-big.great: Great job comparing and ordering numbers!

round-any-place.title: Round Anywhere
round-any-place.description: Round a number to the nearest 10, 100, 1,000, 10,000, or 100,000.
round-any-place.instruction: Round the number to the place shown!

add-sub-multi-digit.title: Add & Subtract Multi-Digit
add-sub-multi-digit.description: Add and subtract big numbers with regrouping.
add-sub-multi-digit.instruction: Solve the problem step by step!

estimate-sum-diff.title: Estimate First
estimate-sum-diff.description: Estimate by rounding, then solve exactly.
estimate-sum-diff.instruction: Estimate by rounding, then solve exactly!
estimate-sum-diff.step3: Step 3: Is the exact answer close to your estimate?
estimate-sum-diff.closeToEstimate: Is the exact answer close to the estimate?
estimate-sum-diff.actuallyNotClose: Actually, {estimate} and {exact} are {diff} apart - that's a bigger gap.

number-line-zoom.title: Number Line Zoom
number-line-zoom.description: Place a number on a zoomed number line.
number-line-zoom.instruction: Tap where the number belongs on the number line!

add-sub-word-problems.title: Two-Step Add/Sub Stories
add-sub-word-problems.description: Solve short stories with big numbers.
add-sub-word-problems.step4: Step 4: What is the final answer?
add-sub-word-problems.whatOperation1: What operation do we use first?
add-sub-word-problems.whatOperation2: What operation do we use next?

balloons.template: {name} has {a} balloons. {pronoun} buys {b} more, then gives {c} away.
balloons.question: How many balloons does {name} have now?

library.template: A library has {a} books. They buy {b} new books, then donate {c} old books.
library.question: How many books does the library have now?

fieldTrip.template: {name} has {a} tickets. {pronoun} sells {b} tickets, then sells {c} more.
fieldTrip.question: How many tickets are left?

stickers.template: {name} collects {a} stickers. {pronoun} trades away {b} stickers and then gets {c} new ones.
stickers.question: How many stickers does {name} have now?

busStops.template: A bus has {a} passengers. At the first stop, {b} people get off. At the second stop, {c} people get on.
busStops.question: How many passengers are on the bus now?

savings.template: {name} saves ${a}. {pronoun} spends ${b} on a project and then earns ${c} doing chores.
savings.question: How much money does {name} have now?

birds.template: There are {a} birds in a park. {b} birds fly away, then {c} more birds arrive.
birds.question: How many birds are in the park now?

candies.template: {name} has {a} candies. {pronoun} gets {b} more from a friend, then eats {c} candies.
candies.question: How many candies does {name} have now?

factor-pairs.title: Factor Pairs Garden
factor-pairs.description: Grow rectangles to find factor pairs.
factor-pairs.instruction: Find all the factor pairs by building rectangles!
factor-pairs.alreadyFound: You already found {rows} × {cols}. Try a new pair!

prime-composite.title: Prime or Composite Sort
prime-composite.description: Sort numbers into prime or composite.
prime-composite.instruction: Sort each number into prime or composite!
prime-composite.hint: Try a rectangle: if it can make a non-1×N rectangle → composite.
prime-composite.numberLabel: Number {number}. Press 1 for Prime, 2 for Composite.

area-model-multiply.title: Area Model Multiply
area-model-multiply.description: Multiply using a rectangle split into tens and ones.
area-model-multiply.instruction: Fill in the area model to find the product!
area-model-multiply.fillAll: Fill in all the boxes first!

partial-products.title: Partial Products Builder
partial-products.description: Build partial products and add them up.
partial-products.instruction: Break apart and multiply, then add!
partial-products.fillAll: Fill in all the boxes first!

divide-place-value.title: Divide with Place Value
divide-place-value.description: Share a big number into equal groups.
divide-place-value.instruction: Divide by taking chunks at each place value!
divide-place-value.chunkHint: Find how many groups are in each chunk.
divide-place-value.fillAll: Fill in all the boxes first!

division-remainders.title: Remainder Stories
division-remainders.description: Decide what the remainder means in a story.
division-remainders.instruction: Read the story and decide what to do with the remainder!
division-remainders.decisionTitle: What does the remainder tell you?

explain.leftover: The remainder shows items left over. That's {remainder} left.
explain.extraGroup: You need one more group because {remainder} are still left.
explain.roundDown: Count only full groups. {remainder} don't fit.

cupcakes.template: {total} cupcakes are packed into boxes of {group}.
cupcakes.question: How many cupcakes are left over?

stickers.template: {total} stickers are packed into bundles of {group}.
stickers.question: How many stickers are left over?

apples.question: How many apples are left over?

buses.template: {total} students are going on a trip. Each bus holds {group} students.
buses.question: How many buses are needed?

tables.template: {total} guests sit at tables with {group} seats each.
tables.question: How many tables are needed?

gift-bags.template: {total} party favors go into bags of {group}.
gift-bags.question: How many bags are needed?

teams.question: How many full teams can they make?

shelves.template: {total} books go on shelves that hold {group} books each.
shelves.question: How many full shelves can be filled?

multi-step-md.title: Multi-Step Multiply/Divide Problems
multi-step-md.description: Solve 1-2 step word problems with multiply and divide.
multi-step-md.instruction: Read carefully and solve step by step!
multi-step-md.step3: Step 3: What is the final answer?
multi-step-md.whatOperation1: What operation do we use first?
multi-step-md.whatOperation2: What operation do we use next?

gardenPots.template: {name} grows {a} trays with {b} flowers each. The flowers are shared equally among {c} neighbors.
gardenPots.question: How many flowers does each neighbor get?

classArtwork.template: A class has {a} drawings for one hallway. They place {b} drawings on each wall. They have {c} identical hallways.
classArtwork.question: How many wall displays do they make in all?

sportsBottles.template: {name} coaches {a} teams with {b} players each. Each player gets {c} water bottles.
sportsBottles.question: How many water bottles are needed?

pizzaSlices.template: A pizza shop bakes {a} trays with {b} slices each. The slices are split into {c} boxes.
pizzaSlices.question: How many slices go in each box?

museumTickets.template: A museum prints {a} tickets for one event day. They pack {b} tickets per bundle. There are {c} event days.
museumTickets.question: How many bundles are there in all?

bookFair.template: The book fair sets up {a} shelves with {b} books on each shelf. The books are shared among {c} classrooms.
bookFair.question: How many books does each classroom get?

gardenFences.template: A gardener builds {a} rows with {b} fence posts each. They build {c} gardens like this.
gardenFences.question: How many fence posts are needed?

marathonMedals.template: A race has {a} medals for one day. They place {b} medals in each case. There are {c} race days.
marathonMedals.question: How many medal cases are needed in all?

compare-fractions.title: Compare Fractions

add-like-fractions.description: Add fractions with the same denominator.

subtract-like-fractions.title: Subtract Like Fractions
subtract-like-fractions.description: Subtract fractions with the same denominator.

mixed-numbers.title: Mixed Numbers Bridge
mixed-numbers.description: Make and use mixed numbers and improper fractions.
mixed-numbers.instruction: Convert between mixed numbers and improper fractions!

fraction-times-whole.title: Fraction x Whole Number
fraction-times-whole.description: Multiply a fraction by a whole number as repeated addition.
fraction-times-whole.instruction: Multiply the fraction by the whole number!

fraction-word-problems.title: Fraction Word Problems
fraction-word-problems.instruction: Read the story and solve with fractions!
fraction-word-problems.prompt: Choose the fraction that solves the story.

recipe.template: You use {a} cup of flour. You add {b} cup more.
recipe.question: How much flour is used?

garden.template: {a} of the garden is planted. You plant {b} more.
garden.question: What fraction is planted now?

pizza.template: You eat {a} of a pizza. You eat {b} more.
pizza.question: How much pizza did you eat?

paint.template: A wall is {a} painted. You paint {b} more.
paint.question: What fraction is painted now?

ribbon.template: A ribbon is {a} meter long. You cut off {b} meter.
ribbon.question: How much ribbon is left?

trail.template: You walk {a} mile out and {b} mile back.
trail.question: How far from the start?

sand.template: A bucket is {a} full of sand. You use {b} of the sand.
sand.question: How much sand is left?

water.template: A pitcher is {a} full. You pour out {b}.
water.question: How full is it now?

decimals-place-value.title: Tenths & Hundredths Aquarium
decimals-place-value.instruction: Shade the grid to show the decimal!
decimals-place-value.hint: Each column is 0.1. Each square is 0.01.
decimals-place-value.columnHint: Tap a top label to shade a whole column (tenths).

compare-decimals.title: Compare Decimals
compare-decimals.description: Choose >, <, or = for decimals to hundredths.
compare-decimals.prompt: Which symbol makes the comparison true?

fraction-decimal-match.instruction: Match each fraction to its decimal!

unit-conversion.mapHint: Use the map to check the conversion factor.

area-perimeter.title: Area & Perimeter Builder
area-perimeter.description: Build rectangles and find area and perimeter.
area-perimeter.instruction: Build a rectangle, then choose the area or perimeter.
area-perimeter.questionArea: What is the area of this rectangle?
area-perimeter.questionPerimeter: What is the perimeter of this rectangle?
area-perimeter.correctArea: Correct! The area is {value} square units.

missing-side.title: Find the Missing Side
missing-side.description: Find a missing side length from area or perimeter.
missing-side.instruction: Use what you know to find the missing side!
missing-side.promptArea: Area = {area} {squareUnits}. One side is {known} {units}. What is the missing side length?
missing-side.promptPerimeter: Perimeter = {perimeter} {units}. One side is {known} {units}. What is the missing side length?
missing-side.shapeAria: Rectangle with one missing side and a known side of {known} {units}

line-plot-fractions.title: Line Plot with Fractions
line-plot-fractions.description: Make and read a line plot with 1/2 and 1/4 units.
line-plot-fractions.instruction: Build the line plot and answer the questions!
line-plot-fractions.buildInstruction: Click on the line plot to add dots!
line-plot-fractions.plotCorrect: ✓ Great line plot! Now answer questions!
line-plot-fractions.plotWrong: Check the data and try again!
line-plot-fractions.questionMost: Which value has the most dots?
line-plot-fractions.questionLeast: Which value has the least dots?
line-plot-fractions.questionTotal: How many dots are there in total?

prompts.uncovered: How much area is uncovered in {unit}?

hints.areaSubtract: Area = length × width. Then subtract the part covered.

ribbon.template: A ribbon is {a} {unit1} long. You add {b} {unit2}.
ribbon.question: What is the total length in {unit2}?

aquarium.template: An aquarium holds {a} {unit1}. You pour out {b} {unit2}.
aquarium.question: How much water is left in {unit2}?

backpack.template: A backpack weighs {a} {unit1}. You add {b} {unit2} of books.
backpack.question: What is the total mass in {unit2}?

blanket.template: A picnic blanket is {a} {unit1} by {b} {unit1}. A mat covers {c} {unit2}.
blanket.question: How much area is uncovered in {unit2}?

board.template: A board is {a} {unit1} long. You cut off {b} {unit2}.
board.question: How long is the board now in {unit2}?

runner.template: A runner jogs {a} {unit1} and then {b} {unit2} more.
runner.question: How far in {unit2}?

ribbonGroup.question: What is the total length in {unit2}?

points-lines-rays.description: Identify points, lines, segments, and rays.
points-lines-rays.hintLine: Goes on forever in both directions.
points-lines-rays.hintRay: Has one endpoint and goes on forever in one direction.

draw-angles.title: Draw an Angle
draw-angles.description: Make an angle of a given size.
draw-angles.instruction: Drag to create the angle shown!

parallel-perpendicular.title: Parallel & Perpendicular Streets
parallel-perpendicular.description: Find parallel and perpendicular lines in a city map.
parallel-perpendicular.instruction: Find the parallel and perpendicular streets!
parallel-perpendicular.correctParallel: Correct! {name1} St and {name2} St are parallel.
parallel-perpendicular.correctPerpendicular: Correct! {name1} St and {name2} St are perpendicular.

classify-quads.title: Classify Quadrilaterals
classify-quads.description: Classify squares, rectangles, rhombi, trapezoids, and kites.
classify-quads.instruction: Sort the quadrilaterals by their properties!

symmetry-lines.title: Symmetry Mirror

symmetry-lines.prompt: Does this shape have a line of symmetry?
symmetry-lines.hint: A line of symmetry divides a shape into two matching halves.

shape-attributes-g4.instruction: Read the clues and pick the right shape!
